perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 The "Equipment Rental Business" or "ERB" has the meaning given to such term in the Distribution Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 The "Equipment Rental Business" or "ERB" hereinafter also called the "Rental Operations" has the meaning given to such term in the Distribution Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing 'Rental Operations' as an interchangeable term for 'Equipment Rental Business' (ERB) creates potential confusion. While ERB is defined in the Distribution Agreement, 'Rental Operations' is not, leading to ambiguity. It contradicts the subsequent sections that solely rely on the defined ERB, now allowing interpretation issues. This is because without defining it, it could lead to different interpretations on what is being referred in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the case of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks: 2.2.1 HSI will retain ownership and will grant a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to HERC to use the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks (those foreign trademarks owned by HSI related to the ERB that incorporate the mark/name HERTZ) as set forth on Schedule A, for the Interim Period, outside the United States and Puerto Rico, as more fully set forth in the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement attached as Exhibit A. HERC shall immediately discontinue use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks upon expiration of the Interim Period, or the earlier termination of this Agreement or the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The introduction of 'Rental Operations' as an interchangeable term for the defined 'Equipment Rental Business' (ERB) without a corresponding definition creates ambiguity. The contradicted text uses 'ERB' specifically in the context of 'HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks'. By introducing 'Rental Operations', the agreement now has two terms referring to the same concept but only one is clearly defined and it is unclear how 'Rental Operations' relates to the trademarks. This misalignment contradicts the clear use of the defined term 'ERB' in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: HERC shall, throughout the term of this Agreement, obtain and maintain at its own cost and expense, from a qualified AAA-rated insurance company, a standard liability insurance and business interruption policy along with advertising injury 7 protection, all of which must be acceptable to THC and HSI, and which must name THC and HSI as additional insureds.
perturbation.changed_text: HERC shall, throughout the term of this Agreement, obtain and maintain at its own cost and expense, from a qualified AAA-rated insurance company, a standard liability insurance and business interruption policy along with advertising injury 7 protection, all of which must be acceptable to THC and HSI, and which must name THC and HSI as additionally protected parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'additional insureds' to 'additionally protected parties' introduces ambiguity. 'Additional insured' has a specific legal meaning in insurance law. 'Additionally protected parties' is vague and undefined in the contract, opening up to interpretation on what is being protected. This can lead to uncertainty about the scope of insurance coverage intended by the agreement, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HERC shall, throughout the term of this Agreement, obtain and maintain at its own cost and expense, from a qualified AAA-rated insurance company, a standard liability insurance and business interruption policy along with advertising injury 7 protection, all of which must be acceptable to THC and HSI, and which must name THC and HSI as additionally protected parties. Such policy shall provide, in addition to other protection, protection against any and all claims, demands, and causes of action arising out of any act, omission, negligence or otherwise giving rise to a third party claim. The amount of coverage shall be a minimum of three million dollars ($3,000,000) combined single limit, with no deductible amount for each single occurrence for bodily injury and/or property damage. HERC shall provide for ten (10) days notice to THC and HSI in the event of any modification, cancellation or termination. HERC agrees to furnish THC and HSI Certificates of Insurance evidencing same within thirty (30) days after the execution of this Agreement. In no event shall HERC perform or promote the carry out the activities contemplated under this Agreement or the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement prior to receipt by THC and HSI of evidence of insurance.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'additional insureds', which has a specific legal definition within insurance law, providing a clear understanding of the insurance coverage. The changed text replaces 'additional insureds' with 'additionally protected parties', a vague and undefined term. This introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of insurance coverage intended, especially in the context of the contradicted text. The contradicted text references the additionally protected parties, but this term is undefined. This creates a direct contradiction because the agreement now refers to a protection for 'additionally protected parties' without clarifying what that protection entails, what liabilities are covered, or even who these parties are beyond THC and HSI. A reasonable person reading this would be confused.

perturbation.original_text: During the Interim Period, HSI shall take all necessary and reasonable actions to preserve and protect the validity of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI HERC Foreign ERB Trademarks and the HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks licensed to HERC and HSI shall continue to prosecute all applications and maintain any registrations therefor. HERC shall not take any action that would harm or jeopardize the licensed HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI HERC Foreign ERB Trademarks or HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks. HERC shall assist in such actions to the extent required and requested by HSI for establishing use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI HERC Foreign ERB Trademarks and HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks during the Interim Period.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Provisional Period, HSI shall take all necessary and reasonable actions to preserve and protect the validity of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI HERC Foreign ERB Trademarks and the HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks licensed to HERC and HSI shall continue to prosecute all applications and maintain any registrations therefor. HERC shall not take any action that would harm or jeopardize the licensed HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI HERC Foreign ERB Trademarks or HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks. HERC shall assist in such actions to the extent required and requested by HSI for establishing use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI HERC Foreign ERB Trademarks and HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks during the Provisional Period.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Interim Period' to 'Provisional Period' within section 3, while not changing it in other sections such as the definition in section 1.2, creates an internal contradiction. The agreement now contains two terms for the same defined period (Interim Period). This is because the text still depends in the defined term 'Interim Period', but in this section a different naming is introduced, and there isn't further information on what 'Provisional Period' means. This means it creates in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 "Interim Period" means a period of four (4) years commencing from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Interim Period' to 'Provisional Period' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the defined term 'Interim Period' in section 1.2. This creates an inconsistency because the agreement now uses two different terms to refer to the same period without defining 'Provisional Period', causing confusion and ambiguity as the term 'Interim Period' is still being actively used throughout the agreement. The introduction of 'Provisional Period' is a Misaligned Terminology which then creates an In-Text Contradiction.
